business.headline: HDFC Bank shares drop after chairman's exit. Should investors buy, sell or wait?HDFC Bank's fall has left investors at a crossroads. With strong fundamentals but lingering uncertainty, the big question now is simple: should you buy the dip, sell, or just wait?

Is global recession just weeks away amid Middle East conflict? Neelkanth Mishra, Chief Economist at Axis Bank, said that around 7% of the world's energy supply is currently affected due to the conflict, which is a major concern for global growth.

No material concerns at HDFC Bank, says RBI after chairman's exitIn a statement, the central bank said it has taken note of the recent developments at HDFC Bank and has approved the transition arrangement proposed by the bank for the position of part-time chairman.

Crude oil prices jump sharply to $111 as Middle East conflict escalatesBrent Crude climbed 3.44% to $111.07 per barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) rose 2.38% to $98.61. Back in India, crude oil prices on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) also went up by around 1%, reaching Rs 9,080 per barrel.

Anil Ambani questioned by CBI in multi-crore State Bank loan 'fraud' caseIndustrialist Anil Ambani will again appear before the CBI tomorrow for questioning in connection with a case registered against Reliance Communications on a complaint by the State Bank of India.

No major issues at HDFC Bank, says Keki Mistry after chairman's sudden exitSpeaking on an investor call, Keki Mistry said there are no major issues at the bank and indicated that the development was not linked to any operational or governance concerns.

Atanu Chakraborty: Retired IAS officer whose abrupt exit from HDFC has raised eyebrowsIn his resignation letter, Atanu Chakraborty flagged concerns over the bank's internal functioning, stating that "certain happenings and practices within the bank" over the past two years were "not in congruence with my personal values and ethics".

HDFC Bank shares tumble 5%. Here's why the stock is down todayThe HDFC Bank stock emerged among the top losers on the benchmark indices, underperforming the broader market amid concerns around governance after Chakraborty stepped down citing issues related to "values and ethics".

Sensex down 1,800 points: What's behind the stock market crash today?The Sensex plunged over 1,800 points and the Nifty fell more than 2% as surging crude oil prices and HDFC Bank concerns triggered a broad-based sell-off across sectors.

Live: 22 Indian ships stuck near Hormuz with LPG, oil cargoLPG crisis live updates: The government has flagged that the LPG supply situation continues to be under pressure and has urged consumers to use cooking gas carefully. At the same time, efforts are underway to expand piped natural gas networks to reduce reliance on LPG. In a major nationwide crackdown, officials said over 12,000 raids have been conducted and more than 15,000 cylinders seized to curb hoarding and black marketing under the Essential Commodities Act. Authorities are also encouraging commercial users in cities to shift to PNG, with simplified connections and incentives being offered. Online LPG bookings have climbed to around 94%, while commercial supplies are gradually resuming in several states with government support. Despite the arrival of fresh shipments like Shivalik and Nanda Devi, the effects of earlier disruptions are still being seen across sectors. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government action and the situation across cities and industries in India.

Sensex, Nifty fall as crude oil surge rattles markets amid Middle East tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex plummeted 1,446.01 points to 75,258.12, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 441.45 points to 23,336.35 as of 9:28 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock market likely to crash today. Here's whyEarly signals from GIFT Nifty suggest a weak start. As of 8:30 am, GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,284, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open well below Wednesday's closing level of 23,777.8.

HDFC Bank shares to be in focus today. Here's whyGlobal brokerage JPMorgan has maintained a "neutral" rating on the stock with a price target of Rs 1,090. HDFC Bank stock is already trading near its 52-week low, adding to weak sentiment around the counter.

Atanu Chakraborty resigns as HDFC Bank part-time chairman over ethics issuesHDFC Bank part-time chairman Atanu Chakraborty resigned citing differences over values and ethics, with the RBI approving Keki Mistry as interim part-time chairman for a three-month period.

US Fed holds rates, flags higher inflation risks amid US-Iran war tensionsFederal Reserve kept rates unchanged, signaled one possible cut in 2026, and projected higher inflation amid Middle East tensions, while markets reacted cautiously and unemployment outlook remained steady.

Reliance Jio hires banks for IPO, no new capital to be raised: ReportThe IPO is expected to be structured as an "offer for sale" (OFS). This means existing investors will sell a part of their stake to the public, rather than the company issuing new shares.

Hidden reasons why life insurance claims get delayed or rejectedFiling a life insurance claim should be a straightforward process, especially during difficult times. Yet, many families face unexpected delays or rejections, often due to small details they overlooked. What really goes wrong?

Russian oil tanker bound for China diverts to India's New Mangalore PortA Russian oil tanker, Aqua Titan, originally bound for China, has diverted course in the South China Sea and is now headed to India's New Mangalore port, set to arrive on March 21st.

7 China-bound Russian oil tankers make U-turn, head to IndiaThe rerouting of these vessels carrying Russian oil comes after India received a temporary waiver from the US to buy 'sanctioned' Russian oil currently stranded at sea.

Iran-US war impact may push up medicine prices in IndiaPharma industry bodies have already raised the issue with the government, sending an emergency report highlighting the risk to raw material supplies.

entertainment.headline: Allu Arjun to Preity Zinta: Celebrities praise Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 Dhurandhar 2 has impressed several celebrities, including Allu Arjun, Kartik Aaryan and Preity Zinta. The film, directed by Aditya Dhar and led by Ranveer Singh, released in the theatres on March 19.

Actors Divyanka Tripathi-Vivek Dahiya announce pregnancy: Plot twist after 10 years Actors Divyanka Tripathi and Vivek Dahiya announced pregnancy, shared that they kept the news private for six months, leaving fans pleasantly surprised.

Ram Gopal Varma says people want to marry Dhurandhar 2 in new remark Ram Gopal Varma has a striking new take on Dhurandhar 2, claiming audiences are deeply invested. His comments are adding to the film's growing buzz—here's why.

Aditya Dhar worked till 2:30 am before Dhurandhar 2 release, reveals Mukesh Chhabra Casting director Mukesh Chhabra praised Aditya Dhar's dedication to Dhurandhar and revealed he worked till 2:30 am before release. He called Dhar a genius and expressed confidence that the film would surpass expectations and break records.

Women National Commission summons Badshah over obscenity in Tateeree song The National Commission for Women has summoned singer Badshah and his team over obscenity in the song Tateeree, raising concerns about its lyrics and visuals under Indian laws.

Ranveer Singh credits Rakesh Bedi for Rs 500 crore of Dhurandhar 2's Rs 1000 crore Rakesh Bedi receives acclaim for his role as Jamali in Dhurandhar 2, with cast and crew highlighting the camaraderie on set and the sequel anticipated to surpass the first film's box office success.

Dhurandhar 2 breaks all premiere records with Rs 52 crore, set for historic run Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has begun its box office journey with record-breaking paid premiere collections exceeding Rs 52 crore.  The film is expected to dominate the box office with positive reviews from preview shows.

Couple Friendly: How this Telugu romantic drama is the warm hug Tollywood needed Couple Friendly is a tender Telugu film that explores young love in all its quiet, messy and real moments. With no loud drama, it taps into the confusion and comfort of modern relationships, with Santosh Sobhan and Manasa Varanasi in lead roles.

Reality show fame Josh Duggar's brother arrested for allegedly molesting 9-year-old Joseph Duggar, brother of Josh Duggar, has been arrested in Florida on charges involving sexual activity with a minor. Details of the investigation and allegations are emerging.

Nora Fatehi's KD song Sarse Ninna Seraga: Was Kannada version tamer than Hindi? The controversial song from the upcoming pan-Indian Kannada film KD - The Devil has drawn criticism for its reportedly vulgar lyrics. While the Hindi version drew maximum backlash, the debate over whether the original Kannada version was equally suggestive has been gaining momentum.

Kim Kardashian stumbles in towering 8-inch heels at Oscars afterparty. Watch Kim Kardashian's Vanity Fair Oscars Party 2026 arrival didn't go entirely as planned. A viral moment in towering heels has everyone talking. Here's what happened.

Divyanka Tripathi confirms pregnancy, says she hid it for six months Divyanka Tripathi confirmed her pregnancy and shared how she kept it a secret for six months. Here's what she shared about the due date, plans and more.

Doraemon director Shibayama Tsutomu dies at 84 of lung cancer Japanese animation figure Shibayama Tsutomu, known for his work on Doraemon, Lupin III, and other popular series, has died at the age of 84. His death was announced by Ajia-do Animation Works, a studio he co-founded, and a private funeral has already taken place.

Dhurandhar The Revenge review: Power, politics and Ranveer Singh who devours it allRelease Date:Wed Mar 18, 2026 Dhurandhar: The Revenge is loud, political, and deeply character-driven, refusing to play safe at any point. Ranveer Singh leads a film that knows exactly the impact it wants to leave behind.

Dhurandhar 2: Madras High Court issues order against illegal streaming of film A day before its scheduled release on March 19, Dhurandhar: The Revenge receives legal protection from the Madras High Court. The order aims to prevent illegal broadcast and streaming across the internet and cable platforms.

Love Insurance Kompany: Pradeep Ranganathan's romantic sci-fi set for April release Vignesh Shivan's Love Insurance Kompany, featuring Pradeep Ranganathan, locks in a new theatrical release date. After multiple delays, the romantic sci-fi drama will hit theatres in April 2026.

Aditya Dhar apologises as Dhurandhar 2 preview screenings falter ahead of release Aditya Dhar apologised for disruptions during Dhurandhar: The Revenge previews, as dubbed versions faced issues. Makers have offered refunds and assured fixes before the March 19 release.

Ram Charan hosts cricketer Tilak Varma on Peddi sets, actor gifts him signed bat T20 World Cup winner Tilak Varma recently visited the sets of Peddi, starring Ram Charan, bringing together cricket and cinema. He was warmly welcomed by the team, adding to the buzz ahead of the film's April 2026 release.

R Madhavan shares UPSC success story of labourer's son ahead of Dhurandhar 2 release R Madhavan shared a video of a Chennai labourer's son who cleared UPSC (Union Public Service Commission) exams at his first attempt. The actor will next be seen in Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

general news.headline: Iran war enters dangerous new phase. India, world may feel the heatThe third week of the Iran war has been marked by repeated targeting of energy assets. After Iran's South Pars gasfield was hit, Tehran retaliated by targeting gas facilities in Qatar and UAE. It signals a dangerous shift in the Iran vs US–Israel war.

BJP's 1st list for Assam polls out: Himanta from Jalukbari, ex-Cong MP from Dispur  Live UpdatesPradyut Bordoloi, the former Congress MP who joined BJP yesterday, will contest from the Dispur assembly constituency.

Can Iran shut down global internet by cutting Hormuz undersea cables?The world is facing an energy crisis as Iran blocked supplies passing through the Strait of Hormuz after strikes by the US and Israel. Vital subsea cables that carry global internet traffic also pass through Hormuz. Any damage to them could trigger internet outages and hit financial systems across countries, including in India.

Is global recession just weeks away amid Middle East conflict? Neelkanth Mishra, Chief Economist at Axis Bank, said that around 7% of the world's energy supply is currently affected due to the conflict, which is a major concern for global growth.

The film is expected to dominate the box office with positive reviews from preview shows.

Grandstanding won't help: Shashi Tharoor backs government's Iran war stanceBacking the Modi government for its strategic silence on the Middle East conflict, Tharoor argued against "moral grandstanding". He said restraint protects national interests and keeps channels of communication open.

No more attacks by Israel unless...: Trump warns Iran after Qatar gas hub strikeTrump sought to distance the US and Qatar from the Israeli strike on Iran's South Pars gas field, and also warned off Tehran from lashing out by attacking the energy infrastructure of Washington's Gulf allies.

No major issues at HDFC Bank, says Keki Mistry after chairman's sudden exitSpeaking on an investor call, Keki Mistry said there are no major issues at the bank and indicated that the development was not linked to any operational or governance concerns.

Watch: 1,000-km-long rain band covers India, Pakistan, AfghanistanThis straight formation is very rare, unlike the usual curved troughs seen in such systems. Such shape and straight structure often builds up into intense thunderstorms with heavy rain, lightning, and strong gusty winds.

Ranveer Singh leads a film that knows exactly the impact it wants to leave behind.

Spitting paan on London street costs two Indian-origin men £2,800 in finesTwo Indian-origin men were fined 1,391 pounds each by authorities in the London borough of Brent after failing to pay an on-spot fine for spitting pan in public and failing to appear in court. Brent City Council spent 30,000 pounds a year on cleaning paan stains and is running a zero-tolerance campaign against paan-spitting in public spaces.

Sensex down 1,800 points: What's behind the stock market crash today?The Sensex plunged over 1,800 points and the Nifty fell more than 2% as surging crude oil prices and HDFC Bank concerns triggered a broad-based sell-off across sectors.

An all-expenses-paid trip to Finland, and there's no catch, honestlyAren't we all, in some way, trying to experience what true happiness really means? Well, the world's happiest country is opening its doors to international travellers for an escape into the Finnish way of life.

Dhurandhar 2 Review, Release Live: Ricky Kej calls Ranveer Singh's film a masterpieceDhurandhar 2 movie review and release live updates: Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge hits theatres amid massive buzz, with early reactions, box office trends and audience response coming in.

Live: Explosions heard in Kuwait, Saudi as drone strikes hit oil refinery, US baseUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The United States was informed in advance of Israel's strike on Iran's South Pars gas field but did not take part, as global oil prices surged on fears of retaliation. Hours later, a missile hit Qatar's key gas facility, causing significant damage and prompting Doha to expel some Iranian embassy officials. Israel also killed Iran's intelligence minister, further destabilising its leadership, as Tehran warned of retaliation. Meanwhile, an Iranian missile struck a building in the occupied West Bank, killing three people and injuring at least 13 — the first fatalities there since the Iran war began. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Ranveer Singh credits Rakesh Bedi for Rs 500 crore of Dhurandhar 2's Rs 1000 croreRakesh Bedi receives acclaim for his role as Jamali in Dhurandhar 2, with cast and crew highlighting the camaraderie on set and the sequel anticipated to surpass the first film's box office success.

Techie lands job in 48 hours at Abu Dhabi startup without applying. All he did was..A techie landed a job at an Abu Dhabi startup within 48 hours after a single GitHub comment, with his viral post talking about the power of open-source contributions and persistence.

FBI hunts Indian national Kalpeshkumar Patel in US-wide fraud scheme caseThe FBI has launched a manhunt for Indian national Kalpeshkumar Patel, accused of running a multi-year fraud scheme that deceived victims into sending cash and prepaid cards across the US.

The million-year accident: Biography of Strait of Hormuz, chokepoint that rules worldBorn of colliding continents and vanished seas, the Strait of Hormuz is the million‑year accident that turned buried oceans into oil, and a thirty‑mile gap in the rocks into the world's most dangerous chokepoint.

Anil Ambani questioned by CBI in multi-crore State Bank loan 'fraud' caseIndustrialist Anil Ambani will again appear before the CBI tomorrow for questioning in connection with a case registered against Reliance Communications on a complaint by the State Bank of India.

Parents wave at CCTV camera every day to check on daughter living away from homeA video posted by Shivani Shukla has received over 4 million views, showing her parents waving and communicating through a CCTV camera she installed to check on them after moving to another city.

She was rejected in interviews. Now she leads 1,000 deaf women building incomesOnce struggling through interviews that ended early due to communication barriers, Kritika Dhunde, a deaf community woman from Nagpur is now leading a powerful network of over 1,000 women across India, helping them build income, and develop confidence and leadership qualities. Her journey shows how accessibility, entrepreneurship and digital visibility can open doors for the deaf community.

Why an Iran at war draws strength from pre-Islamic Persian warriorsImages of pre-Islamic Zoroastrian kings and warriors have been unveiled in Iran, evoking courage and resilience. Many say the Ayatollah regime in Iran revives the country's Persian identity during conflicts with Israel and the US. But this Persian heritage has always run deep, and is visible from time to time, in both dissent and unity.

The sandwich generation layoff: When parents lose jobs and children start earningAs layoffs hit professionals in their early 50s, Indian households are witnessing an unexpected shift. In some families, parents who once carried the financial burden are now relying on their children's first jobs to keep the household afloat

sports.headline: Arjun Tendulkar leaves Rishabh Pant impressed with Sachin-style technique in LSG camp IPL 2026: Arjun Tendulkar has already made heads turn at his new IPL camp. The young all-rounder left LSG captain Rishabh Pant impressed with his bat weight and a technique that clearly reflects his father Sachin Tendulkar's influence.

Galatasaray's Noah Lang suffers freak finger injury vs Liverpool Galatasaray endured a night to forget in the Champions League as injuries piled up during their heavy loss to Liverpool. Noa Lang suffered a freak finger injury while Victor Osimhen picked up a painful arm issue.

Hey Ancelotti, what about me? Neymar's reaction to Brazil squad snub goes viral Neymar's reaction to being snubbed from Brazil's squad for the friendlies against France and Croatia has gone viral as he jokingly asked Carlo Ancelotti why he wasn't in the final list.

Yuzvendra Chahal's shock reveal before IPL 2026: Stopped drinking alcohol Yuzvendra Chahal has revealed that he has decided to keep himself sober and away from alcohol in a bid to get ready for the IPL 2026 season.

Liverpool dismantle Galatasaray, Barcelona hit 7 past Newcastle Liverpool overturned a first-leg deficit to thrash Galatasaray at home to make it to the quarterfinals of the UEFA Champions League. Barcelona dismantled Newcastle and beat them 7-2 to book their spot in the final 8.

IPL 2026: KKR target ex-CSK pace duo as Harshit Rana replacement options Amid the injury concern to Harshit Rana, KKR are targeting ex-CSK pace duo Simarjeet Singh and KM Asif and Sandeep Warrier as potential replacements for the star pacer ahead of the IPL 2026 season.

Iran withdraws FIFA World Cup boycott threat, but refuses to play matches in USA Iran have said that they will not boycott the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026 but remained firm on their stance that they will not play any of the matches in the USA amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

Ishan Kishan keeps rewriting his story, from India outcast to SRH captain IPL 2026: Ishan Kishan faces the challenging task of leading Sunrisers Hyderabad in Pat Cummins' absence, but given the kind of 2026 he has enjoyed so far, all eyes will be on how the Jharkhand batter rises to the occasion in the IPL.

Virat Kohli has a squad of fighters: AB de Villiers backs RCB to defend IPL crown IPL 2026: Former South African batter AB de Villiers backed RCB to win consecutive titles, praising their strong, balanced squad and saying the team is no longer solely reliant on Virat Kohli.

India vs Pakistan on August 19 as FIH unveils Hockey World Cup 2026 schedule India and Pakistan will face off in a highly anticipated pool-stage clash on August 19 at the FIH Hockey World Cup 2026, held across Belgium and the Netherlands from August 15 to 30.

Can SRH survive IPL 2026 without captain Pat Cummins? IPL: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins has been ruled out of the first few matches of the IPL. How big is this blow? Can SRH survive games without their mercurial captain?

Babar Azam, Fakhar Zaman unfit during T20 World Cup selection? PCB source revealsBabar Azam and Fakhar Zaman may not have been fully fit when they were selected for Pakistan's T20 World Cup squad, with the PCB expected to investigate after concerns were raised by senior selector Aaqib Javed and the board's medical panel.

Iran women's football team return home days after seeking asylum in AustraliaThe Iranian women's football team safely returned home from Australia, days after claiming asylum on humanitarian grounds. 5 players withdrew their claim despite earlier fears of persecution amid rising geopolitical tensions.

Spin-friendly pitch can be KKR's key to success in IPL: Faf du PlessisIPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' key to success in the Indian Premier League will depend on the kind of pitches that they make, believes Faf du Plessis. KKR endured a difficult season at home last time.

Ishan Kishan fit enough as T20 captain? Here's what numbers say before IPL 2026IPL 2026: Ishan Kishan has been named Sunrisers Hyderabad's stand-in captain in the absence of Pat Cummins. The left-hander heads into the IPL on the back of a sensational T20 World Cup 2026 campaign.

India drawn with China in Thomas and Uber Cups 2026India have been handed tough draws in both the Thomas Cup and Uber Cup 2026 after being grouped with defending champions China. The BWF announced the draws for the competition, which is set to held  between April 24 and May 3.

FC Barcelona will be Hansi Flick's last coaching jobHansi Flick says Barcelona will be his last club as a coach, ahead of a crucial Champions League second leg against Newcastle at Camp Nou on Wednesday.

SRH name Ishan Kishan as stand-in captain in absence of Pat CumminsIPL 2026: Wicketkeeper batter Ishan Kishan has been named the captain of SunRisers Hyderabad in the absence of Pat Cummins, who is set to miss the first few IPL games for the franchise. Kishan's promotion comes in the backdrop of his sensational outing in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Aryna Sabalenka left fuming, mulls Dubai Championships boycott over harsh criticismMiami Open 2026: World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka has cast doubt over her future participation at the Dubai Tennis Championships after blasting tournament director Salah Tahlak's criticism of player withdrawals as "ridiculous."

Salman Agha, Mehidy Hasan Miraz soar in ICC rankings after stellar ODI seriesSalman Agha and Mehidy Hasan Miraz headlined the latest ICC rankings update after Bangladesh's 2-1 ODI series win over Pakistan, with Salman rising to No.9 among batters and Mehidy climbing to No.7 among bowlers.

Is Bumrah set to lose Rs 2 crore despite T20 World Cup heroics?Fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah was one of the standout performers in India's T20 World Cup campaign. Despite his exceptional outing, sections of the Indian media have reported that he could face a pay cut.

Sadio Mane on Senegal being stripped of AFCON title: Killing the passion of millionsSadio Mané criticises CAF after Senegal lose AFCON 2026 title to Morocco. The decision was made on Tuesday, March 17, to have the title handed over to Morocco after the federation deemed that Senegal forfeited the game.

Lalit Modi's message to SunRisers group: I know a thing or two about managing opticsLalit Modi has offered to help the SunRisers group as their franchise faces heat over signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed for The Hundred 2026.

World Cup teaches you how important mental strength is: Abhishek SharmaIndia opener Abhishek Sharma has opened up on his struggles during the T20 World Cup 2026. The batter has revealed how advice from Yuvraj Singh, Gautam Gambhir and Suryakumar Yadav helped him return to form.

DC captain Axar given warning by Pujara ahead of IPL 2026: Don't be extra casualFormer India cricketer Cheteshwar Pujara told Axar Patel not to get too casual when it comes to being the captain of Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. Axar took over the reins last season for the Capitals.

Despite Ashes axe, Ollie Pope remains hopeful of England Test team returnOllie Pope remains hopeful of an England comeback after being dropped during the Ashes 2025-26, as he aims to regain form in county cricket. Pope admitted that him being dropped during the series against Australia was the right call in the end.

MSD Unplugged: Dhoni is a Man Utd fan with a personal 'Shisha Man'IPL 2026: Sam Billings lifts the lid on MS Dhoni's off-duty habits, revealing his love for Manchester United and his personal shisha man during CSK's IPL campaigns.

Can bat, bowl, field and now fly: Phillips shows pilot skills, video goes viralGlenn Phillips flaunted his skills as a pilot as he landed a small aircraft in Auckland. Phillips has been vocal about his ambition of being a pilot and considers it to be a career option after retiring from cricket.

Koneru Humpy slams Candidates organisers: Can't play if surrounded by bomb sirensIndian chess grandmaster Koneru Humpy has raised serious doubts over her participation at the Candidates tournament in Cyprus, citing safety fears amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

Bangladesh regretting T20 WC snub? Sports ministry to review boycott stanceBangladesh sports minister Anamul Haque orders probe into the country's T20 World Cup boycott and BCB elections, plans talks with ICC over board's future and governance issues. Haque wants to see if there was a failure of sports diplomacy in the call.

King returns to protect the crown, as Kohli touches down in Bengaluru for IPL 2026IPL 2026: The king is back. Virat Kohli has touched down in Bengaluru ahead of IPL 2026, ready to lead RCB's title defence.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
